_comment: Curated ag and economic quotes for AGSIST Daily briefing. Generator picks from this pool — never synthesizes. Fail-loud on missing attribution. Remove any quote that becomes overused; add new ones freely. Structure: {text, attribution, tags}. Tags used for optional contextual selection.

quotes.text: Farming looks mighty easy when your plow is a pencil and you're a thousand miles from the corn field.
The farmer is the only man in our economy who buys everything at retail, sells everything at wholesale, and pays the freight both ways.
Agriculture is our wisest pursuit, because it will in the end contribute most to real wealth, good morals, and happiness.
The discovery of agriculture was the first big step toward a civilized life.
If agriculture goes wrong, nothing else will have a chance to go right.
I believe in the future of agriculture, with a faith born not of words but of deeds.
You have to know the past to understand the present.
Without agriculture it is not possible to have a city, stock market, banks, university, church or army.
The ultimate goal of farming is not the growing of crops, but the cultivation and perfection of human beings.
To forget how to dig the earth and to tend the soil is to forget ourselves.
We abuse land because we regard it as a commodity belonging to us. When we see land as a community to which we belong, we may begin to use it with love and respect.
The soil is the great connector of our lives, the source and destination of all.
Eating is an agricultural act.
A good farmer is nothing more nor less than a handy man with a sense of humus.
Food is the moral right of all who are born into this world.
You can't eat potential.
Take good care of the earth. It was not given to you by your parents. It was loaned to you by your children.
Learn from yesterday, live for today, hope for tomorrow.
The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now.
In seed time learn, in harvest teach, in winter enjoy.
Agriculture is the most healthful, most useful and most noble employment of man.
I'd rather be on my farm than be emperor of the world.
Never invest in a business you cannot understand.
The stock market is a device for transferring money from the impatient to the patient.
Price is what you pay. Value is what you get.
Risk comes from not knowing what you're doing.
In the business world, the rearview mirror is always clearer than the windshield.
The markets can remain irrational longer than you can remain solvent.
When the facts change, I change my mind. What do you do, sir?
The four most dangerous words in investing are: this time it's different.
How did you go bankrupt? Two ways. Gradually, then suddenly.
quotes.attribution: Ernest Hemingway, The Sun Also Rises

quotes.text: If a problem cannot be solved, enlarge it.
Plans are nothing; planning is everything.
The best fertilizer on any farm is the footsteps of the farmer.
Good weather is the greatest gift in farming, and it cannot be purchased.
Don't judge each day by the harvest you reap but by the seeds that you plant.
Hard work beats talent when talent doesn't work hard.
The frost is on the pumpkin and the fodder's in the shock.
It is only the farmer who faithfully plants seeds in the spring, who reaps a harvest in the autumn.
The glory of gardening: hands in the dirt, head in the sun, heart with nature.
The farmer has to be an optimist or he wouldn't still be a farmer.
Buy land, they ain't making any more of it.
The worst thing about being wrong is the lesson you learn.
Good judgment comes from experience, and experience comes from bad judgment.
The more I want to get something done, the less I call it work.
It is better to hear the rebuke of the wise than for a man to hear the song of fools.
To every thing there is a season, and a time to every purpose under the heaven: a time to plant, and a time to pluck up that which is planted.
He that observeth the wind shall not sow; and he that regardeth the clouds shall not reap.
The land is the only thing in the world worth working for, worth fighting for, worth dying for, because it's the only thing that lasts.
quotes.attribution: Gerald O'Hara, Gone With The Wind

quotes.text: The harvest is past, the summer is ended, and we are not saved.
He who plants a tree plants a hope.
Successful farming requires not the abandonment but the creative development of tradition.
A farmer is a magician who produces money from mud.
We are stuck with technology when what we really want is just stuff that works.
Never memorize something that you can look up.
The most dangerous phrase in the language is: we've always done it this way.
Cash is king, but cash flow is the kingdom.
Don't put all your eggs in one basket.
Many a mickle makes a muckle.
Penny wise and pound foolish.
A stitch in time saves nine.
Make hay while the sun shines.
Red sky at night, shepherd's delight; red sky in morning, shepherd's warning.
When March blows its horn, your barn will be filled with hay and corn.
A good farmer never dies; he just goes back to the soil.
The discovery of a new dish confers more happiness on humanity than the discovery of a new star.
Beware of little expenses. A small leak will sink a great ship.
An investment in knowledge pays the best interest.
Well done is better than well said.
By failing to prepare, you are preparing to fail.
Diligence is the mother of good luck.
Lost time is never found again.
It is a mistake to try to look too far ahead. The chain of destiny can only be grasped one link at a time.
However beautiful the strategy, you should occasionally look at the results.
The pessimist sees difficulty in every opportunity. The optimist sees opportunity in every difficulty.
Success is stumbling from failure to failure with no loss of enthusiasm.
You must be the change you wish to see in the world.
A rising tide lifts all boats.
The ability to simplify means to eliminate the unnecessary so that the necessary may speak.
What gets measured gets managed.
The best way to predict the future is to create it.
Culture eats strategy for breakfast.
In God we trust. All others must bring data.
